title: How Cruel Are The Parents.
content:
Tune - "John Anderson, my jo."


I.

        How cruel are the parents
            Who riches only prize,
        And, to the wealthy booby,
            Poor woman sacrifice!
        Meanwhile the hapless daughter
            Has but a choice of strife;
        To shun a tyrant father's hate,
            Become a wretched wife.

II.

        The ravening hawk pursuing,
            The trembling dove thus flies,
        To shun impelling ruin
            Awhile her pinions tries:
        Till of escape despairing,
            No shelter or retreat,
        She trusts the ruthless falconer,
            And drops beneath his feet!
meaning:
"How Cruel Are The Parents." is a quintessential example of Robert Burns's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
